D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

Four Adaptation Transforms

adapt one colour across illuminants through Bradford, CAT02, Sharp, and Von Kries — and measure how far they disagree

A chromatic adaptation transform predicts how a colour shifts when the light changes. Each CAT uses a different cone-response matrix: Von Kries is the 1902 original, Bradford and CAT02 are the modern workhorses, and Sharp uses the most spectrally narrow primaries. They mostly agree — but not exactly.

Choose a hue and the source and destination illuminants. Each patch is the colour adapted by one CAT; the number is its ΔE from the Bradford result. The disagreement grows with the size of the white-point shift.
